_metadata.description: G1 continuity family characters — NA cartoon (S1-S4 + Movie), toy-only, and JP anime (Headmasters, Masterforce, Victory, Zone)

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader. Voiced by Peter Cullen. Killed in the 1986 movie, resurrected in S3

characters.name: Bumblebee
characters.notes: Youngest Autobot. Later reformatted as Goldbug in S3. Voiced by Dan Gilvezan

characters.name: Cliffjumper
characters.notes: Trigger-happy Autobot. Voiced by Casey Kasem

characters.name: Jazz
characters.notes: Voiced by Scatman Crothers

characters.name: Prowl
characters.notes: Autobot strategist. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Ratchet
characters.notes: Autobot medic. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Don Messick

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Optimus Prime's oldest friend. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Peter Cullen

characters.name: Wheeljack
characters.notes: Autobot scientist/inventor. Built the Dinobots with Ratchet. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Chris Latta

characters.name: Mirage
characters.alt_mode: Ligier JS11 Formula One race car
characters.notes: Can turn invisible. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Hound
characters.notes: Autobot scout. Can create holograms. Voiced by Ken Sansom

characters.name: Trailbreaker
characters.notes: Can create force fields. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Sunstreaker
characters.notes: Sideswipe's brother. Yellow. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Sideswipe
characters.notes: Sunstreaker's brother. Red. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Bluestreak
characters.notes: Talkative Autobot. Looks like Prowl but with different colors. Voiced by Casey Kasem

characters.name: Windcharger
characters.notes: Minibot. Has magnetic powers. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by John Stephenson

characters.name: Brawn
characters.notes: Minibot. One of the strongest Autobots for his size. Killed in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Huffer
characters.notes: Minibot. Known for complaining. Voiced by John Stephenson

characters.name: Gears
characters.notes: Minibot. Voiced by Don Messick

characters.name: Hauler
characters.notes: Appears only in S1E01. Never speaks or transforms. Was intended as a 1984 toy but held back and released as Grapple in 1985

characters.name: Skyfire
characters.notes: Former Decepticon scientist and Starscream's friend. Toy sold as Jetfire. Voiced by Gregg Berger

characters.name: Grimlock
characters.notes: Dinobot leader. Voiced by Gregg Berger

characters.name: Slag
characters.notes: Loves fighting. Later renamed Slug in some media due to UK slang. Voiced by Neil Ross

characters.name: Sludge
characters.notes: Least intelligent Dinobot. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Snarl
characters.notes: Created with Swoop as second wave of Dinobots. Voiced by Hal Rayle

characters.name: Swoop
characters.notes: Provides Dinobot air support. Created with Snarl. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Megatron
characters.notes: Decepticon leader. Reformatted into Galvatron in the 1986 movie. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Starscream
characters.notes: Treacherous second-in-command. Killed in the 1986 movie by Galvatron. Returns as ghost in S3. Voiced by Chris Latta

characters.name: Soundwave
characters.notes: Decepticon communications officer. Carries cassette minions. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Thundercracker
characters.notes: Blue Seeker. Voiced by John Stephenson

characters.name: Skywarp
characters.notes: Black/purple Seeker. Can teleport. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Reflector
characters.alt_mode: Camera (three robots combine into one camera)
characters.notes: Three identical robots (Viewfinder, Spectro, Spyglass) that combine into a camera. Generally treated as one character in the cartoon. Voiced by Chris Latta

characters.name: Shockwave
characters.notes: Guardian of Cybertron. Loyal to Megatron. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Laserbeak
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Condor/bird mode. Used extensively for spying. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Ravage
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Jaguar/panther mode. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Rumble
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Humanoid robot with pile-driver arms. Blue in cartoon (red on toy due to color swap with Frenzy). Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Frenzy
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Red in cartoon (blue on toy due to color swap with Rumble). Appears only once in Season 1. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Scrapper
characters.notes: Constructicon leader. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Hook
characters.notes: Constructicon. Forms Devastator's upper body and head. Voiced by Neil Ross

characters.name: Bonecrusher
characters.notes: Constructicon. Voiced by Neil Ross

characters.name: Scavenger
characters.notes: Constructicon. Voiced by Don Messick

characters.name: Long Haul
characters.notes: Constructicon. Voiced by Gregg Berger

characters.name: Mixmaster
characters.notes: Constructicon. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Devastator
characters.notes: Combined form of the six Constructicons. First combiner in Transformers history

characters.name: Bombshell
characters.notes: Insecticon. Uses cerebro-shells for mind control. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Shrapnel
characters.notes: Insecticon. Repeats the last word of his sentences. Voiced by Hal Rayle

characters.name: Kickback
characters.notes: Insecticon. Voiced by Clive Revill

characters.name: Teletraan I
characters.notes: Autobot supercomputer aboard the Ark. Responsible for reformatting Transformers with Earth alt modes

characters.name: Spike Witwicky
characters.notes: Main human protagonist. Son of Sparkplug. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Sparkplug Witwicky
characters.notes: Spike's father. Oil rig worker and mechanic. Voiced by Chris Latta

characters.name: Chip Chase
characters.notes: Wheelchair-bound computer genius. Spike's best friend. Autobot ally. Voiced by Michael Horton

characters.name: Dr. Arkeville
characters.notes: Evil scientist who allies with Megatron. Creates hypno-chips to enslave humans. Turned into cyborg and stranded on Cybertron. Voiced by Casey Kasem

characters.name: Dr. Alcazar
characters.notes: Scientist working on antimatter formula. Minor character. Voiced by John Stephenson

characters.name: Sunstorm
characters.notes: Orange/yellow Seeker from the Welcoming Committee on Cybertron. Unnamed background character in the original airing; retroactively named via e-HOBBY exclusive toy and later media

characters.name: Hotlink
characters.notes: Purple Seeker with flamethrower from the Welcoming Committee on Cybertron. Unnamed background character in the original airing; retroactively named via BotCon 2013

characters.name: Bitstream
characters.notes: Medium-blue Seeker from the Welcoming Committee on Cybertron. Unnamed background character in the original airing; retroactively named via BotCon/Fun Publications

characters.name: Nacelle
characters.notes: Dark blue Seeker barely visible in the Welcoming Committee on Cybertron. Unnamed background character in the original airing; retroactively named via BotCon/Fun Publications

characters.name: Acid Storm
characters.notes: Green Rainmaker Seeker under Shockwave on Cybertron. Can generate acid rain with Nova Storm and Ion Storm. Unnamed in original airing; retroactively named via 2008 Universe toy

characters.name: Nova Storm
characters.notes: Yellow Rainmaker Seeker under Shockwave on Cybertron. Can generate acid rain. Unnamed in original airing; retroactively named

characters.name: Ion Storm
characters.notes: Blue Rainmaker Seeker under Shockwave on Cybertron. Can generate acid rain. Unnamed in original airing; retroactively named

characters.name: Guardian Robot
characters.notes: Giant non-sentient (or semi-sentient) Cybertronian defense robots. Multiple units seen guarding Cybertron. Shockwave commands them. One unit was reprogrammed by Omega Supreme. Called Guardian Robots or Guardians

characters.name: Tracks
characters.notes: Can fly in car mode. Voiced by Michael McConnohie

characters.name: Grapple
characters.notes: Architect. Retool of Inferno mold. Voiced by Peter Renaday

characters.name: Hoist
characters.notes: Maintenance/repair specialist. Retool of Trailbreaker. Voiced by Michael Chain

characters.name: Inferno
characters.notes: Fire truck. Firestar's partner. Voiced by Walker Edmiston

characters.name: Red Alert
characters.notes: Security director. Paranoid personality. Voiced by Michael Chain

characters.name: Smokescreen
characters.notes: Diversionary tactician. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Skids
characters.notes: Theoretician. Rarely appears in the cartoon. Voiced by Michael Chain

characters.name: Powerglide
characters.notes: Only Autobot minibot that flies. Moonracer's partner. Voiced by Michael Chain

characters.name: Warpath
characters.notes: Minibot tank. Punctuates speech with sound effects (BLAM! POW!). Voiced by Alan Oppenheimer

characters.name: Beachcomber
characters.notes: Pacifist geologist. Voiced by Alan Oppenheimer

characters.name: Seaspray
characters.notes: Speaks with a bubbly, gargling voice. Voiced by Alan Oppenheimer

characters.name: Cosmos
characters.notes: Space communications. Lonely due to long solo space missions. Voiced by Michael McConnohie

characters.name: Omega Supreme
characters.alt_mode: Rocket base with tank and rocket
characters.notes: Giant Autobot guardian. Speaks in clipped, terse sentences. Former friend of the Constructicons. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Perceptor
characters.notes: Autobot scientist. Speaks in highly technical language. Voiced by Paul Eiding

characters.name: Blaster
characters.notes: Autobot communications. Counterpart to Soundwave. Loves Earth music. Voiced by Buster Jones

characters.name: Dirge
characters.notes: Conehead Seeker. Engines cause fear in opponents. Voiced by Bud Davis

characters.name: Thrust
characters.notes: Conehead Seeker. Braggart and coward. Voiced by Ed Gilbert

characters.name: Ramjet
characters.notes: Conehead Seeker. Loves to ram things. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Blitzwing
characters.notes: Triple changer. Voiced by Ed Gilbert

characters.name: Astrotrain
characters.alt_mode: JNR Class D51 steam locomotive / Space shuttle
characters.notes: Triple changer. Can alter his size. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Buzzsaw
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Gold condor. Repaint of Laserbeak. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Silverbolt
characters.notes: Aerialbot leader. Afraid of heights. Voiced by Charlie Adler

characters.name: Air Raid
characters.notes: Aerialbot. Reckless. Voiced by Rob Paulsen

characters.name: Fireflight
characters.notes: Aerialbot. Daydreamer. Voiced by Jeff MacKay

characters.name: Skydive
characters.notes: Aerialbot. Studious. Voiced by Laurie Faso

characters.name: Slingshot
characters.notes: Aerialbot. Brash. Voiced by Rob Paulsen

characters.name: Superion
characters.notes: Combined form of the Aerialbots. First Autobot combiner

characters.name: Motormaster
characters.notes: Stunticon leader. King of the Road. Voiced by Roger C. Carmel

characters.name: Drag Strip
characters.alt_mode: Tyrell P34 Formula One race car
characters.notes: Stunticon. Competitive. Voiced by Ron Gans

characters.name: Dead End
characters.notes: Stunticon. Fatalistic. Voiced by Philip L. Clarke

characters.name: Breakdown
characters.notes: Stunticon. Paranoid. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Wildrider
characters.notes: Stunticon. Thrill-seeker. Voiced by Terry McGovern

characters.name: Menasor
characters.notes: Combined form of the Stunticons

characters.name: Onslaught
characters.notes: Combaticon leader. Voiced by Steve Bulen

characters.name: Blast Off
characters.notes: Combaticon. Aloof. Voiced by Milt Jamin

characters.name: Vortex
characters.notes: Combaticon. Interrogator. Voiced by Johnny Haymer

characters.name: Brawl
characters.notes: Combaticon. Loud and angry. Voiced by Steve Bulen

characters.name: Swindle
characters.notes: Combaticon. Arms dealer and con artist. Voiced by Johnny Haymer

characters.name: Bruticus
characters.notes: Combined form of the Combaticons

characters.name: Hot Spot
characters.notes: Protectobot leader. Voiced by Dan Gilvezan

characters.name: Streetwise
characters.notes: Protectobot. Voiced by Peter Cullen

characters.name: Blades
characters.notes: Protectobot. Prefers close combat. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: First Aid
characters.notes: Protectobot medic. Pacifist. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Groove
characters.notes: Protectobot. Peace-loving. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Defensor
characters.notes: Combined form of the Protectobots

characters.name: Alpha Trion
characters.notes: Ancient Autobot sage. Rebuilt Orion Pax into Optimus Prime and Ariel into Elita One. Voiced by John Stephenson

characters.name: Elita One
characters.notes: Female Autobot leader. Optimus Prime's partner. Formerly Ariel. Has time-stopping power. Voiced by Marlene Aragon

characters.name: Chromia
characters.notes: Female Autobot. Ironhide's partner. Elita One's second-in-command

characters.name: Moonracer
characters.notes: Female Autobot. Sharpshooter. Powerglide's partner. Impetuous

characters.name: Firestar
characters.notes: Female Autobot. Inferno's partner

characters.name: Orion Pax
characters.notes: Optimus Prime's original identity before being rebuilt by Alpha Trion. Voiced by Peter Cullen

characters.name: Ariel
characters.notes: Elita One's original identity before being rebuilt by Alpha Trion

characters.name: Dion
characters.notes: Orion Pax and Ariel's friend. Presumably killed by Megatron in War Dawn. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Devcon
characters.notes: Autobot bounty hunter. Only appears in one episode. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Nightbird
characters.notes: Human-built female ninja robot. Reprogrammed by the Decepticons. Not a Transformer

characters.name: Kremzeek
characters.notes: Living electrical creature accidentally created by Megatron. Annoying. Can replicate

characters.name: TORQ III
characters.notes: Supercomputer reprogrammed by Megatron to control Earth's machines

characters.name: Autobot X
characters.notes: Spare Autobot body into which Spike's mind is transferred. Goes berserk

characters.name: Vector Sigma
characters.notes: Ancient Cybertronian supercomputer that gives life to Transformers. Guarded by Centurion droids

characters.name: Carly
characters.notes: Spike's girlfriend (later wife). Skilled engineer. Mother of Daniel. Voiced by Arlene Banas

characters.name: Astoria Carlton-Ritz
characters.notes: Heiress to Hybrid Technologies. Develops a crush on Powerglide. Voiced by Linda Gary

characters.name: Raoul
characters.notes: Street-smart young man from New York. Tracks' human friend. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Shawn Berger
characters.notes: Corrupt politician who conspires with Megatron to discredit the Autobots. Voiced by Paul Eiding

characters.name: Abdul Fakkadi
characters.notes: Leader of Carbombya. Parody of Muammar Gaddafi. Recurring. Voiced by Philip L. Clarke

characters.name: Dr. Fujiyama
characters.notes: Japanese robotics scientist who created Nightbird. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Lord Chumley
characters.notes: British big game hunter who targets Optimus Prime. Voiced by Philip L. Clarke

characters.name: Auggie Cahnay
characters.notes: Race car driver featured in the Stunticons episode

characters.name: Ali
characters.notes: Young boy who befriends the Aerialbots in Carbombya

characters.name: Alana
characters.notes: Alien female from Tlalakan. Seaspray's love interest. Can shapeshift

characters.name: Deceptitran
characters.notes: Alien tyrant ruling Tlalakan. Decepticon ally

characters.name: Hot Rod
characters.notes: Young Autobot warrior. Becomes Rodimus Prime after opening the Matrix. Voiced by Judd Nelson

characters.name: Rodimus Prime
characters.notes: Hot Rod's evolved form after receiving the Matrix of Leadership. New Autobot leader. Voiced by Judd Nelson

characters.name: Ultra Magnus
characters.notes: City commander of Autobot City. Chosen by Optimus Prime to carry the Matrix. Killed by Galvatron, rebuilt by Junkions. Voiced by Robert Stack

characters.name: Arcee
characters.notes: Female Autobot warrior. Prominent in Season 3. Voiced by Susan Blu

characters.name: Springer
characters.notes: Autobot Triple Changer. Cocky warrior. Voiced by Neil Ross

characters.name: Kup
characters.notes: Grizzled veteran Autobot. Tells war stories constantly. Voiced by Lionel Stander

characters.name: Blurr
characters.notes: Fastest Autobot. Speaks extremely rapidly. Voiced by John Moschitta Jr.

characters.name: Wheelie
characters.notes: Small Autobot found on Quintessa. Speaks in rhyme. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Galvatron
characters.notes: Megatron reformatted by Unicron. New Decepticon leader. Driven insane. Voiced by Leonard Nimoy (movie), Frank Welker (Season 3)

characters.name: Cyclonus
characters.notes: Galvatron's most loyal lieutenant. Created by Unicron from either Bombshell or Skywarp (debated). Voiced by Roger C. Carmel

characters.name: Scourge
characters.notes: Decepticon tracker. Created by Unicron from Thundercracker. Leader of the Sweeps. Voiced by Stan Jones

characters.name: Unicron
characters.notes: Planet-sized Transformer. Chaos bringer. Devours worlds. Destroyed by the Matrix. Voiced by Orson Welles in his final film role

characters.name: Wreck-Gar
characters.notes: Junkion leader. Speaks in TV catchphrases and commercial jingles. Voiced by Eric Idle

characters.name: Quintesson Judge
characters.notes: Multi-faced Quintesson who presides over mock trials. Sentences all defendants to death. Voiced by Regis Cordic

characters.name: Quintesson Prosecutor
characters.notes: Quintesson who conducts prosecutions in the Quintessa courts

characters.name: Kranix
characters.notes: Last survivor of Lithone (planet destroyed by Unicron). Tells Hot Rod and Kup about Unicron before being executed by Sharkticons. Voiced by Norm Alden

characters.name: Arblus
characters.notes: Lithonian killed during Unicron's attack on Lithone. Appears in the movie's opening scene. Voiced by Norm Alden

characters.name: Daniel Witwicky
characters.notes: Son of Spike and Carly Witwicky. Child. Prominent in Season 3 and 4. Voiced by David Mendenhall

characters.name: Metroplex
characters.notes: Autobot city-bot. Transforms between city, battle station, and giant robot. Counterpart to Trypticon. Voiced by Bud Davis

characters.name: Sky Lynx
characters.notes: Arrogant Autobot. Splits into shuttle (bird) and ground transport (lynx). Arch-enemy of the Predacons. Voiced by Aron Kincaid

characters.name: Scamper
characters.notes: Metroplex's autonomous car drone

characters.name: Six-Gun
characters.notes: Metroplex's autonomous robot drone

characters.name: Slammer
characters.notes: Metroplex's autonomous tank drone

characters.name: Sandstorm
characters.notes: Autobot Triple Changer. Voiced by Jerry Houser

characters.name: Broadside
characters.notes: Autobot Triple Changer. Afraid of heights and gets seasick. Voiced by Jack Angel

characters.name: Steeljaw
characters.notes: Blaster's cassette minion. Lion mode

characters.name: Ramhorn
characters.notes: Blaster's cassette minion. Rhinoceros mode

characters.name: Eject
characters.notes: Blaster's cassette minion. Humanoid robot

characters.name: Rewind
characters.notes: Blaster's cassette minion. Humanoid robot

characters.name: Outback
characters.notes: Minibot. Retool of Brawn. Voiced by Gregg Berger

characters.name: Pipes
characters.notes: Minibot. Retool of Huffer

characters.name: Swerve
characters.notes: Minibot. Retool of Gears

characters.name: Tailgate
characters.notes: Minibot. Retool of Windcharger

characters.name: Hubcap
characters.notes: Minibot. Retool of Cliffjumper

characters.name: Goldbug
characters.notes: Bumblebee rebuilt and renamed. Throttlebot. Voiced by Dan Gilvezan

characters.name: Chase
characters.notes: Throttlebot

characters.name: Freeway
characters.notes: Throttlebot

characters.name: Rollbar
characters.notes: Throttlebot

characters.name: Searchlight
characters.notes: Throttlebot

characters.name: Wideload
characters.notes: Throttlebot

characters.name: Scattershot
characters.notes: Technobot leader. Created by Grimlock. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Lightspeed
characters.notes: Technobot

characters.name: Nosecone
characters.notes: Technobot

characters.name: Strafe
characters.notes: Technobot

characters.name: Afterburner
characters.notes: Technobot

characters.name: Computron
characters.notes: Combined form of the Technobots. Extremely intelligent but slow to act due to analyzing all possibilities

characters.name: Trypticon
characters.notes: Decepticon city-bot. Transforms between city, battle station, and T-Rex. Counterpart to Metroplex. Built by Constructicons for the Quintessons. Voiced by Brad Garrett

characters.name: Octane
characters.notes: Decepticon Triple Changer. Defects and becomes an outcast. Voiced by Beau Weaver

characters.name: Razorclaw
characters.notes: Predacon leader. Voiced by Joe Leahy

characters.name: Divebomb
characters.notes: Predacon. Swoop's old rival from Cybertron

characters.name: Headstrong
characters.notes: Predacon

characters.name: Rampage
characters.notes: Predacon

characters.name: Tantrum
characters.notes: Predacon

characters.name: Predaking
characters.notes: Combined form of the Predacons. Considered one of the most powerful combiners

characters.name: Hun-Gurrr
characters.notes: Terrorcon leader. Insatiably hungry. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Sinnertwin
characters.notes: Terrorcon

characters.name: Blot
characters.notes: Terrorcon. Extremely unclean

characters.name: Cutthroat
characters.notes: Terrorcon

characters.name: Rippersnapper
characters.notes: Terrorcon

characters.name: Abominus
characters.notes: Combined form of the Terrorcons. Rival of Computron

characters.name: Ratbat
characters.notes: Soundwave's cassette minion. Bat mode. Used for energy auditing and spying

characters.name: Runabout
characters.notes: Battlecharger. Paired with Runamuck

characters.name: Runamuck
characters.notes: Battlecharger. Paired with Runabout

characters.name: Gnaw
characters.notes: Named Sharkticon. Quintesson enforcer. Toy name used to represent the generic Sharkticon species

characters.name: Marissa Faireborn
characters.notes: Captain in the Earth Defense Command (EDC). Daughter of Flint and Lady Jaye from G.I. Joe. Recurring character. Voiced by Susan Blu

characters.name: Gregory Swofford
characters.notes: Human scientist who discovers Optimus Prime's body and uses it as bait in a revenge plot. Voiced by Barry Gordon

characters.name: Jessica Morgan
characters.notes: Human scientist injured by Decepticons. Gregory Swofford's partner in the revenge plot. Voiced by Susan Blu

characters.name: Primacron
characters.notes: Ancient alien scientist who created Unicron and Tornedron. Voiced by Rege Cordic

characters.name: Primacron's Assistant
characters.notes: Oracle-like entity who summons the Primitives to fight Tornedron. Formerly Primacron's lab assistant. Voiced by Hal Rayle

characters.name: Tornedron
characters.notes: Energy creature created by Primacron. Drains all energy it encounters. Destroyed by Grimlock

characters.name: Dirk Manus
characters.notes: Human space trader. Pilot of the Lazy Sue. Voiced by Dick Gautier

characters.name: Skuxxoid
characters.notes: Mercenary alien race. Recurring minor characters used by Quintessons. Generic species name used as character name

characters.name: Fortress Maximus
characters.notes: Largest Autobot. Transforms into city and battle station. Head is Cerebros, whose head is Spike. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Cerebros
characters.notes: Intermediate Headmaster. Transforms into Fortress Maximus's head. His own head is Spike Witwicky

characters.name: Chromedome
characters.notes: Autobot Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Stylor. Voiced by Frank Welker

characters.name: Hardhead
characters.notes: Autobot Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Duros. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Highbrow
characters.notes: Autobot Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Gort. Voiced by Johnny Haymer

characters.name: Brainstorm
characters.notes: Autobot Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Arcana. Voiced by Michael Bell

characters.name: Stylor
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Chromedome. Appearance-obsessed

characters.name: Duros
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Hardhead. Old-fashioned soldier

characters.name: Gort
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Highbrow. Farmer. Voiced by Johnny Haymer

characters.name: Arcana
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Brainstorm. Scientist

characters.name: Scorponok
characters.notes: Decepticon Headmaster city-bot. Counterpart to Fortress Maximus. Nebulan partner: Lord Zarak. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Mindwipe
characters.notes: Decepticon Headmaster. Hypnotist. Nebulan partner: Vorath

characters.name: Skullcruncher
characters.notes: Decepticon Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Grax

characters.name: Weirdwolf
characters.notes: Decepticon Headmaster. Speaks with inverted syntax. Nebulan partner: Monzo

characters.name: Lord Zarak
characters.notes: Nebulan leader who becomes Scorponok's Headmaster partner. Leader of the Hive. Primary Nebulan antagonist. Voiced by Stephen Keener

characters.name: Vorath
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Mindwipe. Scientist

characters.name: Grax
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Skullcruncher

characters.name: Monzo
characters.notes: Nebulan partner of Weirdwolf

characters.name: Pointblank
characters.notes: Autobot Targetmaster. Nebulan weapon partner: Peacemaker

characters.name: Sureshot
characters.notes: Autobot Targetmaster. Nebulan weapon partner: Spoilsport

characters.name: Crosshairs
characters.notes: Autobot Targetmaster. Nebulan weapon partner: Pinpointer

characters.name: Peacemaker
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Pointblank. Transforms into a gun

characters.name: Spoilsport
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Sureshot. Transforms into a gun

characters.name: Pinpointer
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Crosshairs. Transforms into a gun

characters.name: Triggerhappy
characters.notes: Decepticon Targetmaster. Nebulan weapon partner: Blowpipe

characters.name: Misfire
characters.notes: Decepticon Targetmaster. Terrible aim. Nebulan weapon partner: Aimless

characters.name: Slugslinger
characters.notes: Decepticon Targetmaster. Nebulan weapon partner: Caliburst

characters.name: Blowpipe
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Triggerhappy. Lord Zarak's brother-in-law. Transforms into a compressed-air cannon

characters.name: Aimless
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Misfire. Transforms into a gun

characters.name: Caliburst
characters.notes: Nebulan weapon partner of Slugslinger. Transforms into a gun

characters.name: Sixshot
characters.alt_mode: Cybertronian jet / laser pistol / armored carrier / winged wolf / tank / robot
characters.notes: Six-changer — one of only two Transformers with six modes. Decepticon ninja. Voiced by Neil Ross

characters.name: Fastlane
characters.notes: Autobot Clone. Identical robot mode to Cloudraker

characters.name: Cloudraker
characters.notes: Autobot Clone. Identical robot mode to Fastlane

characters.name: Pounce
characters.notes: Decepticon Clone. Identical robot mode to Wingspan

characters.name: Wingspan
characters.notes: Decepticon Clone. Identical robot mode to Pounce

characters.name: Battletrap
characters.alt_mode: Helicopter + off-road vehicle (two vehicles merge into one robot)
characters.notes: Duocon. Two vehicles that combine into one robot

characters.name: Flywheels
characters.alt_mode: Jet + tank (two vehicles merge into one robot)
characters.notes: Duocon. Two vehicles that combine into one robot

characters.name: Apeface
characters.notes: Headmaster Horrorcon. Triple changer. Nebulan partner: Spasma

characters.name: Snapdragon
characters.notes: Headmaster Horrorcon. Triple changer. Nebulan partner: Krunk

characters.name: Spasma
characters.notes: Nebulan Headmaster partner of Apeface

characters.name: Krunk
characters.notes: Nebulan Headmaster partner of Snapdragon

characters.name: Punch-Counterpunch
characters.notes: Autobot double agent. Robot mode switches between Autobot (Punch) and Decepticon (Counterpunch) identities

characters.name: Llyra
characters.notes: Lord Zarak's daughter. Sympathetic to the Autobots

characters.name: Camshaft
characters.notes: Omnibot. Mail-order exclusive via Robot Points. Triple changer (car/armed car/robot). Diaclone Double Changer mold

characters.name: Downshift
characters.notes: Omnibot. Mail-order exclusive. Triple changer (car/armed car/robot). Diaclone Double Changer mold

characters.name: Overdrive
characters.notes: Omnibot. Mail-order exclusive. Triple changer (car/armed car/robot). Diaclone Double Changer mold

characters.name: Aragon
characters.notes: Powerdasher. Mail-order exclusive. Pull-back motor. Name from Diaclone; never officially named in Transformers. Drill type

characters.name: Cromar
characters.notes: Powerdasher. Mail-order exclusive. Pull-back motor. Name from Diaclone. Jet type

characters.name: Zetar
characters.notes: Powerdasher. Mail-order exclusive. Pull-back motor. Name from Diaclone. Car type

characters.name: Barrage
characters.notes: Deluxe Insecticon. Bandai/Takatoku mold — never appeared in cartoon due to licensing conflict with Takara. Excellent articulation for era

characters.name: Chop Shop
characters.notes: Deluxe Insecticon. Bandai mold. Can disassemble into multiple insect components

characters.name: Ransack
characters.notes: Deluxe Insecticon. Bandai mold. Not to be confused with Kickback (standard Insecticon also a grasshopper)

characters.name: Venom
characters.notes: Deluxe Insecticon. Bandai mold. Leader of the Deluxe Insecticons

characters.name: Roadbuster
characters.notes: Deluxe Vehicle. Takatoku Dorvack mold. Never in cartoon due to licensing; prominent in Marvel UK comics as a Wrecker. Heavy die-cast, many accessories

characters.name: Whirl
characters.notes: Deluxe Vehicle. Takatoku Dorvack mold. Never in cartoon; prominent Wrecker in Marvel UK comics. Later became major IDW character

characters.name: Topspin
characters.notes: Jumpstarter. Pull-back auto-transform gimmick. Animation model exists but never appeared in cartoon. Prominent Wrecker in Marvel comics

characters.name: Twin Twist
characters.notes: Jumpstarter. Pull-back auto-transform gimmick. Never in cartoon despite having an animation model. Wrecker in Marvel comics

characters.name: Backstreet
characters.notes: Triggerbot. Flip-out gun gimmick in both modes. Budget price point. Appeared in Marvel UK comics

characters.name: Dogfight
characters.notes: Triggerbot. Flip-out gun gimmick

characters.name: Override
characters.notes: Triggerbot. Flip-out gun gimmick

characters.name: Crankcase
characters.notes: Triggercon. Flip-out gun gimmick. Appeared in Marvel comics

characters.name: Ruckus
characters.notes: Triggercon. Flip-out gun gimmick

characters.name: Windsweeper
characters.notes: Triggercon. Flip-out gun gimmick

characters.name: Fizzle
characters.notes: Sparkabot. Friction-spark gimmick. Budget price point. Appeared in Marvel UK comics

characters.name: Guzzle
characters.notes: Sparkabot. Friction-spark gimmick. Later gained popularity via IDW comics

characters.name: Sizzle
characters.notes: Sparkabot. Friction-spark gimmick

characters.name: Cindersaur
characters.notes: Firecon. Friction-spark gimmick. Only Firecon to get subsequent toy updates (BotCon, Power of the Primes)

characters.name: Flamefeather
characters.notes: Firecon. Friction-spark gimmick

characters.name: Sparkstalker
characters.notes: Firecon. Friction-spark gimmick

characters.name: Getaway
characters.notes: Powermaster. Nebulan engine partner: Rev. JP counterpart is Lightfoot (different character in Masterforce)

characters.name: Joyride
characters.notes: Powermaster. Nebulan engine partner: Hotwire. JP counterpart toy was recolored as Ranger (different character)

characters.name: Slapdash
characters.notes: Powermaster. Nebulan engine partner: Lube. JP counterpart is Road King (different character)

characters.name: Dreadwind
characters.notes: Powermaster. Nebulan engine partner: Hi-Test. Combines with Darkwing to form Dreadwing. JP counterpart is Buster (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Darkwing
characters.notes: Powermaster. Nebulan engine partner: Throttle. Combines with Dreadwind to form Dreadwing. JP counterpart is Hydra (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Dreadwing
characters.notes: Combined jet form of Dreadwind and Darkwing. JP counterpart is Darkwings (Masterforce). Not to be confused with later characters of the same name

characters.name: Doubledealer
characters.notes: Powermaster double agent. Two Nebulan partners: Knok (Autobot) and Skar (Decepticon). Can assume Autobot or Decepticon robot modes. JP counterpart is Doubleclouder (Masterforce)

characters.name: Quickswitch
characters.alt_mode: Drill tank / jet / hovercraft / pistol / puma / robot
characters.notes: Autobot Six Changer. Son of Sixshot (per some bios). JP counterpart is Sixknight (Masterforce, different character and different colors)

characters.name: Landfill
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Flintlock and Silencer

characters.name: Quickmix
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Boomer and Ricochet

characters.name: Scoop
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Holepunch and Tracer

characters.name: Needlenose
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Sunbeam and Zigzag

characters.name: Quake
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Tiptop and Heater

characters.name: Spinister
characters.notes: Double Targetmaster. Two Nebulan weapon partners: Hairsplitter and Singe. Gained popularity in IDW comics

characters.name: Nightbeat
characters.notes: Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Muzzle. Detective personality. Major character in Marvel US/UK comics. JP mold recolored as Minerva (different character)

characters.name: Hosehead
characters.notes: Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Lug. JP mold used for Cab (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Siren
characters.notes: Headmaster. Nebulan partner: Quig. JP mold recolored as Go Shooter (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Fangry
characters.notes: Headmaster beast. Nebulan partner: Brisko. JP counterpart is Wilder (Masterforce, different character — a human teenager)

characters.name: Horri-Bull
characters.notes: Headmaster beast. Nebulan partner: Kreb. JP counterpart is Bullhorn (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Squeezeplay
characters.notes: Headmaster beast. Nebulan partner: Lokos. JP counterpart is Cancer (Masterforce, different character)

characters.name: Cloudburst
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. JP counterpart is Phoenix (Masterforce)

characters.name: Groundbreaker
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Landmine
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. JP counterpart is Lander (Masterforce)

characters.name: Sky High
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Splashdown
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Waverider
characters.notes: Pretender with human warrior shell. JP counterpart is Diver (Masterforce)

characters.name: Gunrunner
characters.notes: Pretender Vehicle. Vehicle shell transforms. No JP release

characters.name: Bomb-Burst
characters.notes: Pretender with vampire bat monster shell. JP counterpart is Blood (Masterforce)

characters.name: Bugly
characters.notes: Pretender with insect monster shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Finback
characters.notes: Pretender with fish monster shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Iguanus
characters.notes: Pretender with lizard monster shell. No JP counterpart

characters.name: Skullgrin
characters.notes: Pretender with skull monster shell. JP counterpart is Dauros (Masterforce). Briefly became an actor in Marvel comics

characters.name: Submarauder
characters.notes: Pretender with fish monster shell. JP counterpart is Gilmer (Masterforce)

characters.name: Roadgrabber
characters.notes: Pretender Vehicle. Vehicle shell transforms. No JP release

characters.name: Catilla
characters.notes: Pretender Beast. Beast shell with inner Transformer robot. Featured in Marvel UK comics. No JP release

characters.name: Chainclaw
characters.notes: Pretender Beast. Beast shell with inner Transformer. No JP release

characters.name: Carnivac
characters.notes: Pretender Beast. Defected to Autobots in Marvel UK comics (Survivors storyline). Fan favorite. No JP release

characters.name: Snarler
characters.notes: Pretender Beast. No JP release

characters.name: Grand Slam
characters.notes: Autobot cassette. Tank mode. Combines with Raindance to form Slamdance

characters.name: Raindance
characters.notes: Autobot cassette. Jet mode. Combines with Grand Slam to form Slamdance

characters.name: Slamdance
characters.notes: Combined form of Grand Slam and Raindance. First and only combining cassette team

characters.name: Beastbox
characters.notes: Decepticon cassette. Gorilla mode. Combines with Squawktalk to form Squawkbox

characters.name: Squawktalk
characters.notes: Decepticon cassette. Bird mode. Combines with Beastbox to form Squawkbox

characters.name: Squawkbox
characters.notes: Combined form of Beastbox and Squawktalk

characters.name: Bumblebee Pretender
characters.notes: Classic Pretender version of Bumblebee. Simplified inner robot in human Pretender shell. Also sold without shell as 'Legends' Kmart exclusive

characters.name: Grimlock Pretender
characters.notes: Classic Pretender version of Grimlock. Simplified inner robot in human Pretender shell

characters.name: Jazz Pretender
characters.notes: Classic Pretender version of Jazz. Simplified inner robot in human Pretender shell

characters.name: Starscream Pretender
characters.notes: Classic Pretender version of Starscream. Simplified inner robot in human Pretender shell

characters.name: Vroom
characters.notes: Mega Pretender. Vehicle shell that robot rides in. Shell also transforms

characters.name: Crossblades
characters.notes: Mega Pretender. Vehicle shell transforms. JP mold remolded as Blue Bacchus (Victory)

characters.name: Thunderwing
characters.notes: Mega Pretender. Shell transforms into jet. Major Marvel US comics character — wielded the Matrix. Highly sought-after figure

characters.name: Skyhammer
characters.notes: Ultra Pretender. Both inner robot and outer shell transform. Prone to gold plastic syndrome breakage

characters.name: Roadblock
characters.notes: Ultra Pretender. Both inner robot and shell transform. Very rare complete due to gold plastic syndrome

characters.name: Birdbrain
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Small Pretender that combines. Forms right arm of Monstructor

characters.name: Bristleback
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Forms left leg of Monstructor. Prone to gold plastic syndrome

characters.name: Icepick
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Forms torso of Monstructor

characters.name: Scowl
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Forms right leg of Monstructor

characters.name: Slog
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Forms left arm of Monstructor. Very prone to GPS breakage

characters.name: Wildfly
characters.notes: Pretender Monster. Forms back/wings of Monstructor

characters.name: Monstructor
characters.notes: Combined form of the six Pretender Monsters. Only Pretender combiner. Complete sets extremely expensive due to gold plastic syndrome. JP counterpart: Dinoking (Victory, recolored with dinosaur shells)

characters.name: Bludgeon
characters.notes: Pretender with skeleton samurai shell. Fan favorite. Major Marvel comics character. Shell is iconic — became leader of Decepticons in comics

characters.name: Octopunch
characters.notes: Pretender with diving suit/tentacle monster shell

characters.name: Stranglehold
characters.notes: Pretender with barbarian warrior shell

characters.name: Doubleheader
characters.notes: Pretender with two-headed warrior shell

characters.name: Longtooth
characters.notes: Pretender with walrus-man shell

characters.name: Pincher
characters.notes: Pretender with beetle warrior shell

characters.name: Shouki
characters.notes: Trainbot leader. Elder brother of Yukikaze. Japanese name: Shōki (翔輝)

characters.name: Getsuei
characters.notes: Trainbot. Japanese name: Getsuei (月影)

characters.name: Kaen
characters.notes: Trainbot. Japanese name: Kaen (火炎)

characters.name: Seizan
characters.notes: Trainbot. Japanese name: Seizan (星山)

characters.name: Suiken
characters.notes: Trainbot. Japanese name: Suiken (水拳)

characters.name: Yukikaze
characters.notes: Trainbot. Younger brother of Shouki. Japanese name: Yukikaze (雪風)

characters.name: Raiden
characters.notes: Combined form of the six Trainbots. Only G1 combiner with six components (instead of the usual five). Japanese name: Raiden (ライデン)

characters.name: Twincast
characters.notes: Blaster rebuilt after his fatal duel with Soundwave. Upgraded with dual cassette deck. Japanese name: Twincast (ツインキャスト)

characters.name: Soundblaster
characters.notes: Soundwave rebuilt by Scorponok after his fatal duel with Blaster. Black body. Japanese name: Soundblaster (サウンドブラスター)

characters.name: Grotusque
characters.notes: Monsterbot. Breathes fire. Japanese name: Grotus (グロタス)

characters.name: Doublecross
characters.notes: Monsterbot. Two heads argue with each other. Japanese name: Doublecross (ダブルクロス)

characters.name: Repugnus
characters.notes: Monsterbot. Japanese name: Repug (リパッグ)

characters.name: Slugfest
characters.notes: Soundblaster's cassette minion. Stegosaurus mode. Japanese name: Slugfest (スラッグフェスト)

characters.name: Overkill
characters.notes: Soundblaster's cassette minion. Tyrannosaurus mode. Japanese name: Overkill (オーバーキル)

characters.name: Snaptrap
characters.notes: Seacon leader. Japanese name: Turtler (タートラー). Seacons briefly appear in Headmasters finale

characters.name: Overbite
characters.notes: Seacon. Japanese name: Gulf (ガルフ)

characters.name: Seawing
characters.notes: Seacon. Japanese name: Seawing (シーウィング)

characters.name: Skalor
characters.notes: Seacon. Japanese name: Skalor (スカラー)

characters.name: Tentakil
characters.notes: Seacon. Japanese name: Tentakil (テンタキル)

characters.name: Nautilator
characters.notes: Seacon. Transforms into Piranacon's weapon rather than a limb. Japanese name: Lobclaw (ロブクロー)

characters.name: Piranacon
characters.notes: Combined form of the Seacons. Six members (Nautilator becomes weapon). Japanese name: King Poseidon (キングポセイドン)

characters.name: Metalhawk
characters.notes: Leader of the Autobot Pretenders. Disguises as human 'Hawk'. Has been on Earth for 8,000 years. Japanese name: Metalhawk (メタルホーク)

characters.name: Lander
characters.notes: Autobot Pretender. Lives in New York as a socialite. Western toy counterpart: Landmine. Japanese name: Lander (ランダー)

characters.name: Diver
characters.notes: Autobot Pretender. Introduces Ginrai to the Autobots. Western toy counterpart: Waverider. Japanese name: Diver (ダイバー)

characters.name: Phoenix
characters.notes: Autobot Pretender. Gets minimal screen time. Western toy counterpart: Cloudburst. Japanese name: Phoenix (フェニックス)

characters.name: Blood
characters.notes: Leader of the Decepticon Pretenders. Vampire bat monster form. Sealed in Egyptian pyramids. Western toy counterpart: Bomb-Burst. Japanese name: Blood (ブラッド)

characters.name: Dauros
characters.notes: Decepticon Pretender. Dinosaur monster form. Sealed beneath Nazca Lines. Western toy counterpart: Skullgrin. Japanese name: Dauros (ドーロス)

characters.name: Gilmer
characters.notes: Decepticon Pretender. Fish monster form. Sealed in Atlantis. Western toy counterpart: Submarauder. Japanese name: Gilmer (ギルマー)

characters.name: Ginrai
characters.notes: 19-year-old Japanese-American truck driver from Nagano. Main protagonist. Transtector identical to Optimus Prime. Becomes Super Ginrai (with trailer), then God Ginrai (with Godbomber). NOT Optimus Prime — entirely different character. Japanese name: Ginrai (ジンライ)

characters.name: Lightfoot
characters.notes: British Godmaster. Son of a scientist. Western toy counterpart: Getaway. Japanese name: Lightfoot (ライトフット)

characters.name: Ranger
characters.notes: Canadian park ranger Godmaster. Japanese-exclusive redeco of Joyride. Japanese name: Ranger (レンジャー)

characters.name: Road King
characters.notes: French Godmaster. Western toy counterpart: Slapdash. Japanese name: Road King (ロードキング)

characters.name: Giga
characters.notes: Male half of the Decepticon Godmaster duo. Husband of Mega. Together they form Overlord. Japanese name: Giga (ギガ)

characters.name: Mega
characters.notes: Female half of the Decepticon Godmaster duo. Wife of Giga. Together they form Overlord. Japanese name: Mega (メガ)

characters.name: Overlord
characters.notes: Combined Godmaster form of Giga (tank) and Mega (jet). Primary Decepticon field commander. Extremely powerful. Japanese name: Overlord (オーバーロード)

characters.name: Buster
characters.notes: German Godmaster. Brother of Hydra. Together they form Darkwings. Western toy counterpart: Dreadwind. Japanese name: Buster (バスター)

characters.name: Hydra
characters.notes: German Godmaster. Brother of Buster. Together they form Darkwings. Western toy counterpart: Darkwing. Japanese name: Hydra (ハイドラー)

characters.name: Darkwings
characters.notes: Combined jet form of Buster and Hydra. Japanese name: Darkwings (ダークウィングス)

characters.name: Doubleclouder
characters.notes: Last Godmaster discovered. Double agent — can adopt both Autobot and Decepticon robot modes. Sides with Decepticons. Western toy counterpart: Doubledealer. Japanese name: Doubleclouder (ダブルクラウダー)

characters.name: Shuta Go
characters.notes: 13-year-old Japanese boy. Son of Professor Go. Robot form is Go Shooter. Western toy counterpart: Siren. Japanese name: Shūta Gō (周太)

characters.name: Cab
characters.notes: Orphaned boy from the Karin Islands. Has a parrot and armadillo. Robot form is Cab. Western toy counterpart: Hosehead. Japanese name: Cab (キャブ)

characters.name: Minerva
characters.notes: French teenage girl. Combat medic. Breakout character of Masterforce. Japanese-exclusive redeco; western counterpart is Nightbeat. Japanese name: Minerva (ミネルバ)

characters.name: Wilder
characters.notes: Delinquent teenage boy. Western toy counterpart: Fangry. Japanese name: Wilder (ワイルダー)

characters.name: Cancer
characters.notes: Chinese teenage boy. Eventually defects to help Autobots. Close friend of Browning. Western toy counterpart: Squeezeplay. Japanese name: Cancer (キャンサー)

characters.name: Bullhorn
characters.notes: Delinquent teenage boy. Western toy counterpart: Horri-Bull. Japanese name: Bullhorn (ブルホーン)

characters.name: Godbomber
characters.notes: Non-sentient Autobot drone that combines with Super Ginrai to form God Ginrai. Empathic weapon. Japanese-exclusive. Japanese name: Godbomber (ゴッドボンバー)

characters.name: Sixknight
characters.alt_mode: Cybertronian car / jet / submarine / drill tank / winged wolf / gun
characters.notes: Six-changer Autobot warrior. Member of the Six Clan like Sixshot. Initially a lone warrior, joins the Autobots. Western toy counterpart: Quickswitch. Japanese name: Sixknight (シックスナイト)

characters.name: Grand Maximus
characters.notes: Fortress Maximus's younger brother. Has a Pretender shell for his Headmaster component Grand. Japanese-exclusive repaint of Fortress Maximus. Japanese name: Grand Maximus (グランドマキシマス)

characters.name: Browning
characters.notes: Small Decepticon who transforms into a handgun. Given to Cancer as a companion. Loyal and childlike. Japanese-exclusive. Japanese name: Browning (ブローニング)

characters.name: Black Zarak
characters.notes: Scorponok's transtector rebuilt and powered by Devil Z. Merges with Devil Z for the final battle. Black repaint of Scorponok. Japanese name: BlackZarak (ブラックザラック)

characters.name: Devil Z
characters.notes: Super-energy alien being. True leader of the Decepticons in Masterforce. Exists as a pulsating globe of energy. Merges with Black Zarak for the final battle. Japanese name: Devil Z (デビルZ)

characters.name: Star Saber
characters.notes: Autobot Supreme Commander. Brainmaster. Small jet (Saber) docks with larger booster jet (V-Star). Adoptive father of Jan Minakaze. Armed with the Saber Blade. Japanese name: Star Saber (スターセイバー)

characters.name: Victory Leo
characters.notes: God Ginrai's transtector reformatted after being fatally wounded by Deathsaurus. Savage but powerful. Combines with Star Saber to form Victory Saber. Japanese name: Victory Leo (ビクトリーレオ)

characters.name: Victory Saber
characters.notes: Combined form of Star Saber and Victory Leo. Most powerful Autobot in the Victory series. Japanese name: Victory Saber (ビクトリーセイバー)

characters.name: Blacker
characters.notes: Brainmaster leader. Star Saber's second-in-command. Forms Road Caesar's torso. Japanese name: Blacker (ブラッカー)

characters.name: Braver
characters.notes: Brainmaster. Japanese name: Braver (ブレイバー)

characters.name: Laster
characters.notes: Brainmaster. Japanese name: Laster (ラスター)

characters.name: Road Caesar
characters.notes: Combined form of Blacker, Braver, and Laster. Three-member combiner (unusual). Japanese name: Road Caesar (ロードシーザー)

characters.name: Wing
characters.notes: Multiforce leader. Can pair-combine with any Multiforce member. Usually forms Wingwaver with Waver. Japanese name: Wing (ウイング)

characters.name: Waver
characters.notes: Multiforce member. Usually pair-combines with Wing to form Wingwaver. Japanese name: Waver (ウェーバー)

characters.name: Dash
characters.notes: Multiforce member. Usually pair-combines with Tacker to form Dashtacker. Japanese name: Dash (ダッシュ)

characters.name: Tacker
characters.notes: Multiforce member. Usually pair-combines with Dash to form Dashtacker. Japanese name: Tacker (タッカー)

characters.name: Mach
characters.notes: Multiforce member. Usually pair-combines with Tackle to form Machtackle. Japanese name: Mach (マッハ)

characters.name: Tackle
characters.notes: Multiforce member. Usually pair-combines with Mach to form Machtackle. Japanese name: Tackle (タックル)

characters.name: Landcross
characters.notes: Combined form of all six Multiforce members. Unique: any two members can pair-combine, and all six form Landcross. Japanese name: Landcross (ランドクロス)

characters.name: Greatshot
characters.alt_mode: Tank / jet / car / drill tank / winged wolf / gun
characters.notes: Six-changer samurai. Member of the Six Clan (like Sixshot and Sixknight). Retool of Sixshot toy. Japanese name: Greatshot (グレートショット)

characters.name: Galaxy Shuttle
characters.notes: Large Autobot who transports the team. Carries the Brainmasters and Multiforce into battle. Japanese name: Galaxy Shuttle (ギャラクシーシャトル)

characters.name: Holi
characters.notes: Rescue Patrol leader. Child-like personality. Close friend of Jan. Japanese name: Holi (ホーリー)

characters.name: Fire
characters.notes: Rescue Patrol member. Japanese name: Fire (ファイアー)

characters.name: Boater
characters.notes: Rescue Patrol member. Japanese name: Boater (ボーター)

characters.name: Pipo
characters.notes: Rescue Patrol member. Japanese name: Pipo (ピーポ)

characters.name: Deathsaurus
characters.notes: Decepticon Emperor of Destruction. Has two Breast Animals (tiger and eagle) that transform into weapons. Star Saber's nemesis. Commands the Thunder Arrow warship and the Planet-Destroying Fortress. Japanese name: Deszaras (デスザラス)

characters.name: Leozack
characters.notes: Breastforce leader. Scheming and ambitious; plots to usurp Deathsaurus. Lion breast animal. Japanese name: Leozack (レオザック)

characters.name: Drillhorn
characters.notes: Breastforce member. Rhino breast animal. Japanese name: Drillhorn (ドリルホーン)

characters.name: Killbison
characters.notes: Breastforce member. Buffalo breast animal. Japanese name: Killbison (キルバイソン)

characters.name: Hellbat
characters.notes: Breastforce member. Sneaky and self-serving. Bat breast animal. Japanese name: Hellbat (ヘルバット)

characters.name: Gaihawk
characters.notes: Breastforce member. Last to join (imprisoned on Planet Micro). Hawk breast animal. Japanese name: Gaihawk (ガイホーク)

characters.name: Jaruga
characters.notes: Breastforce member. Also spelled Jaguar/Jallguar. Jaguar breast animal. Sometimes forms weapon for Liokaiser rather than a limb. Japanese name: Jaruga (ジャルガ)

characters.name: Liokaiser
characters.notes: Combined form of all six Breastforce members. Can turn invisible. Most powerful combiner in Victory. Japanese name: Liokaiser (ライオカイザー)

characters.name: Goryu
characters.notes: Dinoforce commander. Pretender with T-Rex shell. Japanese name: Gōryū (剛龍)

characters.name: Kakuryu
characters.notes: Dinoforce member. Pretender with Triceratops shell. Japanese name: Kakuryū (角龍)

characters.name: Rairyu
characters.notes: Dinoforce member. Pretender with Brontosaurus shell. Japanese name: Rairyū (雷龍)

characters.name: Doryu
characters.notes: Dinoforce member. Pretender with Stegosaurus shell. Japanese name: Doryū (土龍)

characters.name: Gairyu
characters.notes: Dinoforce member. Pretender with Pteranodon shell. Japanese name: Gairyū (凱龍)

characters.name: Yokuryu
characters.notes: Dinoforce member. Pretender with Plesiosaur shell. Becomes weapon for Dinoking. Japanese name: Yokuryū (翼龍)

characters.name: Dinoking
characters.notes: Combined form of all six Dinoforce members. Bumbling but dangerous. Japanese name: Dinoking (ダイノキング)

characters.name: Jan Minakaze
characters.notes: Star Saber's adopted human son. Orphaned when his parents were killed in a Decepticon attack. Japanese name: Jan Minakaze (ジャン南風)

characters.name: Illumina
characters.notes: Human woman. Jan's classmate's teacher. Briefly befriended by Autobots. Japanese name: Illumina (イルミナ)

characters.name: Deathcobra
characters.notes: Briefly considered by Leozack as Hellbat's replacement in Breastforce. Japanese name: Deathcobra (デスコブラ)

characters.name: Dai Atlas
characters.notes: New Autobot Supreme Commander after Victory Saber is wounded. Triple changer with motorized gimmick. Armed with Zone Sword. Has a son named Speeder. Can transform to 'Zone Mode' powered by the Zodiac. Japanese name: Dai Atlas (ダイアトラス)

characters.name: Sonic Bomber
characters.notes: Dai Atlas's combining partner and deputy commander. Can transform to 'Zone Mode' powered by the Zodiac. Japanese name: Sonic Bomber (ソニックボンバー)

characters.name: Sky Powered
characters.notes: Combined form of Dai Atlas and Sonic Bomber. Can reach Mach 17. Defeats Predaking with ease. Japanese name: Sky Powered (スカイパワード)

characters.name: Roadfire
characters.notes: Third Powered Master. Only appears in TV Magazine story pages, not in the animated OVA. Defeats Metrotitan with a single 'Fire Kick'. Japanese name: Roadfire (ロードファイアー)

characters.name: Rabbicrater
characters.notes: Commander of the Micromaster Rocket Base. Known as Countdown in the US. Moon rabbit motif. Japanese name: Rabbicrater (ラビットクレーター)

characters.name: Moonradar
characters.notes: Galaxy Shuttle pilot. Tracks Decepticon movements with radar. Japanese name: Moonradar (ムーンレーダー)

characters.name: Speeder
characters.notes: Dai Atlas's son. Pairs with Dai Atlas. Japanese name: Speeder (スピーダー)

characters.name: Sonic
characters.notes: Pairs with Sonic Bomber. Japanese name: Sonic (ソニック)

characters.name: Violengiguar
characters.notes: Decepticon Emperor of Destruction. Mysterious three-faced insectoid entity who commands the Nine Great Demon Generals. Also known as Violen Jiger. Creates the 'Decepticon Zone'. Japanese name: Violengiguar (ヴァイオレンジガー)

characters.name: Metrotitan
characters.notes: Repainted Metroplex created beneath Titan. Taught in the ways of evil by the Nine Generals. Accompanied by the Metrosquad. Only in story pages, not in OVA. Japanese name: Metrotitan (メトロタイタン)

characters.name: Cain
characters.notes: Boy from planet Zone. Befriends Akira. Has a pet rabbit named Emusa. Japanese name: Cain (カイン)

characters.name: Emusa
characters.notes: Cain's pet rabbit from Feminia. Last survivor of Feminia alongside Cain. Japanese name: Emusa (エムーサ)

characters.name: Akira
characters.notes: Human boy who befriends Cain. His father taught him about the Zodiac. Japanese name: Akira Serikawa (芹川アキラ)
